Description: Arcturus Therapeutics Holdings Inc. is a biotechnology company based in San Diego, California, founded in 2013. The company specializes in the discovery, development, and commercialization of RNA medicines and vaccines targeting rare diseases, respiratory conditions, and infectious diseases. Arcturus utilizes advanced nucleic acid therapeutics and has developed proprietary platforms such as LUNAR® for RNA delivery and STARR® for self-amplifying mRNA technology. The company has made significant strides in its product pipeline, including KOSTAIVE® (ARCT-021), the first approved self-amplifying mRNA COVID-19 vaccine, authorized in Japan and the European Union. Arcturus is also developing therapies for rare diseases like ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency and has candidates for cystic fibrosis and influenza. With over 500 patents and applications worldwide, Arcturus is well-positioned in the biotechnology sector, supported by a diverse and experienced team.

Description: LevoStar Bio is a biotechnology company that specializes in the production and testing of cell and gene therapy products, offering groundbreaking technologies and comprehensive solutions to the cell and gene therapy industry. The company's scope of business includes traceability compliance services, plasmid and mRNA production, CGT analytical testing services, and ready-to-use products, providing full-process coverage services from compliant synthesis to plasmid/mRNA production.

Company Name: Precision NanoSystems is now part of Cytiva
Description: Precision NanoSystems, based in Vancouver, Canada, is a leader in technologies and solutions for developing and manufacturing nanomedicines, particularly lipid nanoparticles (LNPs) for genetic medicines such as mRNA vaccines. The company became part of Cytiva, a Danaher Corporation Life Sciences platform company, in June 2021, enhancing its capabilities in advanced therapeutics and gene editing. The company offers a comprehensive portfolio that includes pre-optimized LNP formulations, a library of custom lipids, and advanced formulation technologies. These products support drug development from early stages through clinical and commercial phases. Precision NanoSystems provides full workflow solutions, including analytical and regulatory support, and collaborates with partners like Aldevron and Replicate Bioscience to streamline mRNA programs and nanomedicine development. With a focus on innovation, it serves over 200 pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies across more than 20 countries.

Description: RaNA Therapeutics, founded in 2011, was a biotechnology company dedicated to developing RNA-targeted medicines aimed at promoting healthy gene expression for genetic diseases. In early 2017, it rebranded as Translate Bio to better reflect its mission of enhancing therapeutic protein production through RNA platforms. The company was based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and was co-founded by scientist Arthur Krieg. Translate Bio focused on mRNA-based therapeutics, particularly through its messenger RNA therapy (MRT) platform. This platform was expanded with the acquisition of Shire plc's MRT technology, enabling the development of treatments for conditions such as cystic fibrosis and ornithine transcarbamylase (OTC) deficiency. The company raised significant funding before its acquisition, emphasizing its commitment to addressing rare genetic diseases through innovative RNA science.

Description: Recipharm Advanced Bio is a global contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) that specializes in advanced therapy medicinal products (ATMPs). As a division of Recipharm, it offers comprehensive services from preclinical development to commercial manufacturing. The company focuses on innovative biotherapeutics, including xRNA therapies, viral vectors, live biotherapeutic products, recombinant protein vaccines, plasmid DNA, and lipid nanoparticles. With facilities in Watertown, MA, Cuxhaven, Germany, and Portugal, Recipharm Advanced Bio supports a range of capabilities in biological modalities. Its services include process development, manufacturing, cell banking, and sterile fill-finish, all conducted in EU GMP-certified and FDA-licensed environments. The company emphasizes integrated operations to streamline project timelines and minimize risks, ensuring efficient delivery of therapies to patients worldwide. Recipharm Advanced Bio collaborates with leading innovators to enhance its offerings and contribute to global health advancements.

Description: Moderna, Inc. is a biotechnology company based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, founded in 2010. The company specializes in messenger RNA (mRNA) technology to develop medicines and vaccines for various conditions, including infectious diseases, cancer, and rare genetic disorders. Originally named ModeRNA Therapeutics, Moderna has gained significant recognition for its COVID-19 vaccine, mRNA-1273, which was authorized for emergency use in December 2020. Moderna's innovative mRNA platform allows for the rapid development of treatments and vaccines by instructing cells to produce therapeutic proteins. The company has a diverse pipeline that includes candidates for infectious diseases like influenza and Zika, personalized cancer vaccines in collaboration with Merck, and treatments for rare diseases such as cystic fibrosis. Moderna has established partnerships with several pharmaceutical companies and agencies to advance its mRNA research and expand its therapeutic offerings. The company operates a manufacturing facility in Norwood, Massachusetts, and continues to grow its research capabilities.

Description: The Alliance for mRNA Medicines (AMM) is a global organization dedicated to advancing mRNA and next-generation RNA therapeutics and vaccines. Launched on November 1, 2023, at the 11th International mRNA Health Conference in Berlin, AMM was founded by 31 biotechnology, biopharma, and life sciences companies and educational institutions. Its mission is to improve patients' lives and enhance scientific knowledge by bringing together industry leaders, innovators, and scientists. AMM focuses on five key areas: regulatory frameworks, standards development, education, access, and pandemic preparedness. The organization advocates for policies that support innovation and patient access to mRNA medicines across North America, Europe, and Asia. It offers services such as policy advocacy, scientific standards development, public education initiatives, industry networking, and support for research advancement in various mRNA applications. Founding members include notable organizations like BioNTech, CureVac, and Johns Hopkins University, with leadership provided by Andy Geall, the chairman of the AMM board.

Description: Virogin Biotech is a clinical-stage immuno-oncology company based in Vancouver, Canada. The company focuses on developing innovative therapies using HSV-1 based oncolytic virotherapy and mRNA-based therapeutics. Virogin aims to create impactful drugs for patients with significant medical needs, utilizing a combined oncolytic virus and mRNA vaccine therapy approach to enhance anti-tumor immune responses against solid tumors. The company's pipeline includes six oncolytic virus candidates, four mRNA vaccines, two therapeutic vaccines, two prophylactic vaccines, and one gene therapy. Notable assets include VG161, an oncolytic virus in Phase 2 trials for hepatocellular carcinoma, and several mRNA candidates in various stages of development targeting different solid tumors. Virogin's platforms are designed to work together, providing flexibility for independent development against cancer and other indications.

Description: ReCode Therapeutics is a clinical-stage genetic medicines company based in Menlo Park, Texas, founded in 2015. The company focuses on developing targeted therapies for genetically defined diseases, particularly those with limited or no treatment options. ReCode utilizes a proprietary Selective Organ Targeting (SORT) lipid nanoparticle platform, which enhances the delivery of mRNA and gene correction therapies to organs beyond the liver. The company's pipeline primarily targets rare respiratory diseases, including cystic fibrosis and primary ciliary dyskinesia. Their lead programs employ SORT LNP technology for mRNA therapy and gene correction, aiming to address the underlying causes of these conditions. ReCode is committed to advancing innovative solutions in genetic medicine, leveraging its expertise in lipid nanoparticle and nucleic acid technologies.
